company_name: LG Energy Solution initial LFP $4.3B contract disclosure cap
notes: Initial 2025 disclosure value and duration support Stage 2, but customer and use-case were undisclosed then, so Stage 3-Green must be capped.
The same $4.3B LFP contract becomes a stronger Stage 2 candidate once Tesla Megapack 3 and Lansing production are confirmed, but ramp-up, utilization, ESS OPM, and FCF remain required.
GWh and duration support Stage 2, but missing contract value caps EPS/FCF and Green credit.
EV CAPA can become hard 4C while a separate ESS conversion remains only a Watch candidate.

company_name: SK On-Ford battery JV dissolution and ESS pivot watch
notes: JV dissolution can reduce fixed-cost drag and create an ESS pivot option, but EV-demand weakness and operating losses keep it Watch.

company_name: Ford Energy ESS and AI data-center storage pivot
notes: Ford Energy is a useful AI data-center storage pivot reference, but exact source date, customer contracts, margins, and FCF remain unverified.
Long-term EV battery supply contracts are not automatically Green if customer EV strategy changes.
A multi-trillion-won EV battery order can still be 4C if the customer cancels and expected revenue disappears.
Recycling gets stronger when recovered metals, ESS/grid services, data-center demand, and customers connect.

company_name: Second-life battery grid storage SOH validation reference
notes: Second-life storage can become useful grid evidence only when SOH, safety, warranty, and customer contracts are independently validated.

company_name: Battery graphite supply security with cost risk
notes: China dependency makes graphite strategically important, but US cost, policy financing, and offtake must be validated before Green.
Waste treatment is R3's clearest Green-capable infrastructure reference when permits, volume, and recurring FCF are visible.
Actual CAPEX is stronger than policy talk, but customers, utilization, and OP conversion are still required.
Solar subsidy and US manufacturing narrative can break when customs and component supply stop production.
Renewable policy and PPAs cannot overcome project economics when rates, foundations, and delay create impairment.
Lithium rebound is not structural Green without cost curve, offtake, FCF, and CAPEX discipline.
ESS can improve lithium demand, but lithium miners remain cycle/Watch until FCF and supply response are controlled.

company_name: Sodium-ion substitution pressure on lithium/LFP economics
notes: Sodium-ion is a price-ceiling and substitution overlay for lithium/LFP cases, not a positive Green target.

company_name: Korea EV battery certification and fire risk
notes: EV fire and battery disclosure should become a separate RedTeam overlay for battery, charging, and ESS candidates.

company_name: Moss Landing BESS fire safety and permitting overlay
notes: Large BESS demand can be offset by fire, permitting, insurance, and local-safety risk; exact stage date needs backfill.

company_name: Battery SOH transparency and second-life risk
notes: Second-life and recycling economics can be overstated when SOH and residual capacity are not independently validated.
A solid-state license can be Stage 2, but Green waits for production, vehicle adoption, royalty revenue, cost, and yield.
Solid-state and new battery materials stay Watch/Red until commercialization, customers, production, margin, and FCF are proven.
